title: Antalya 5-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Antalya 5-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Antalya, Turkey: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Aspendos Roman Theatre and Düden Waterfalls boat tour. Prices from $1150 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Antalya, Turkey — Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (June-August, highs 33-36°C) perfect for the beach, and mild, wetter winters (December-February, highs around 15°C) with occasional rain. The sea stays swimmable May through October. May-June and September-October offer warm, sunny weather ideal for beaches and sightseeing without the intense heat and peak crowds of July-August. This itinerary blends Aspendos Roman Theatre, Düden Waterfalls boat tour, Kaleiçi (old town) streets with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Aspendos Roman Theatre, Düden Waterfalls boat tour, Kaleiçi (old town) streets
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Antalya
itinerary.description: Land at Antalya Airport (AYT) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Aspendos Roman Theatre — One of the best-preserved Roman theatres in the world, still used for performances. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Antalya: Düden Waterfalls boat tour & Antalya Aquarium
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Düden Waterfalls boat tour — Boat trip to see the falls cascade directly into the Mediterranean. In the afternoon, continue to Antalya Aquarium — One of the world's longest underwater tunnel aquariums. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Antalya: Perge ancient city ruins & Antalya cable car (Tünektepe)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Perge ancient city ruins — Extensive Greco-Roman ruins including a stadium and colonnaded street. In the afternoon, continue to Antalya cable car (Tünektepe) — Panoramic views over the city, mountains, and coastline. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Perge and Aspendos ancient cities
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Perge and Aspendos ancient cities, a popular side trip from Antalya. Return to Antalya in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Perge and Aspendos ancient cities

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Köprülü Canyon white-water rafting or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to AYT for your departure flight.

travelTips: The Antray tram is the easiest way to get from the airport to Kaleiçi and the beaches.
Visit Aspendos and Perge early morning to avoid both the heat and tour-group crowds.
Bargain at Kaleiçi bazaars — starting around 30-40% below the asking price is normal.
Book Köprülü Canyon rafting trips in advance during July-August peak season.
The sea is warm and swimmable from late May through October.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable shoes for Kaleiçi's cobblestones and ancient ruin sites
A light cover-up for mosque visits
Light layers for cooler spring/autumn evenings
thingsToDo: Scuba diving Kaş's underwater sites (day trip)
The Land of Legends theme park
thingsToAvoid: Walking Kaleiçi's cobblestones in flip-flops or heels — sturdy shoes make a real difference.
Visiting major ruin sites (Aspendos, Perge) at midday in summer without sun protection.
Overpaying at tourist-facing restaurants directly on the marina without checking a menu with prices first.
Assuming taxi meters are always used — confirm the driver starts the meter or agree a fare upfront.
topRestaurants: Seraser Fine Dining — Ottoman & Mediterranean Fine Dining ($$$$)
Kaleiçi kebab houses — Turkish Kebab & Grill ($)
Antalya seafront meyhanes — Turkish Meze & Seafood ($$)
instagramSpots: Kaleiçi harbor with the old town backdrop
Düden Waterfalls cliff drop into the sea
Konyaaltı Beach with Taurus Mountains behind
hiddenGems: Olympos ancient ruins and Chimaera eternal flames
Side's quieter Temple of Apollo at sunset
familyActivities: The Land of Legends theme park
adventureActivities: Scuba diving Kaş's underwater sites (day trip)
luxuryExperiences: Private yacht charter along the Turkish Riviera
Spa day at a 5-star Belek resort
Fine dining tasting menu at Seraser
Private guided tour of Aspendos and Perge
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner cruise from Antalya Marina
Private beach cabana at a Belek resort
Romantic stroll through Kaleiçi's lantern-lit lanes
budgetExperiences: Free walking tour of Kaleiçi and Hadrian's Gate
Local kebab and çay for just a few dollars
localEtiquette: A polite greeting and small talk before business is customary, even in shops and taxis.
Modest dress is appreciated when visiting mosques (headscarves for women, covered shoulders/knees for all).
Turkish hospitality often includes offers of tea (çay) — accepting is polite, even briefly.
Bargaining is expected and enjoyed in the bazaars of Kaleiçi, but not in fixed-price stores.
Remove shoes when entering a mosque or a local home.
localTransportGuide: Antalya has an efficient tram (Antray) connecting the airport, city center, and Konyaaltı Beach. Dolmuş (shared minibuses) cover routes buses don't, and taxis are metered and affordable. Most beach resorts along the coast (Belek, Side, Lara) are more easily reached by rental car or organized transfer.
bestTimeToVisit: May-June and September-October offer warm, sunny weather ideal for beaches and sightseeing without the intense heat and peak crowds of July-August.
weather: Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (June-August, highs 33-36°C) perfect for the beach, and mild, wetter winters (December-February, highs around 15°C) with occasional rain. The sea stays swimmable May through October.
visaInformation: US, Canadian, and Australian citizens generally need an e-Visa (obtainable online in minutes); most EU/UK citizens can enter visa-free for short stays. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~9.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Antalya?
faqs.answer: US, Canadian, and Australian citizens generally need an e-Visa (obtainable online in minutes); most EU/UK citizens can enter visa-free for short stays. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Antalya?
faqs.answer: May-June and September-October offer warm, sunny weather ideal for beaches and sightseeing without the intense heat and peak crowds of July-August. Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (June-August, highs 33-36°C) perfect for the beach, and mild, wetter winters (December-February, highs around 15°C) with occasional rain. The sea stays swimmable May through October.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Antalya safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Antalya is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Antalya?
faqs.answer: The local currency is TRY. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Antalya Aquarium.

reviews.author: Tom Larsen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Antalya Aquarium and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Anna Ivanov
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Antalya is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Antalya cable car (Tünektepe). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Daniel Costa
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Aspendos Roman Theatre and Antalya cable car (Tünektepe). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Mohammed Rossi
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Antalya Marina promenade alone was worth the trip, but combined with Antalya Marina promenade this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Lukas Tanaka
